title: make_transport breaks for IPv6 bracket addresses
description: Trigger: ?server=[::1]:8080 or WOLFHSM_SERVER=[::1]:8080. rsplit_once(':') splits correctly (host=[::1], port=8080) but wolfhsm Transport::Tcp passes the ip string directly to the OS; most TCP connect implementations reject '[::1]' (with brackets). Result: connect fails with a confusing OS error instead of an InvalidUri. Standard notation for IPv6 in URL authority is host:port where host is bracketed, but wolfhsm expects a bare address.
